symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, painless breast lump (patient self-palpated) present ~2 months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard, painless, persistent breast lump is a cardinal malignancy feature.
Fibroadenomas are typically mobile and soft, not hard and fixed.
Spontaneous unilateral bloody nipple discharge is an alarm feature for malignancy.
Papilloma is the classic benign cause of bloody discharge, but cannot be relied upon here given the mass and nodes.
New nipple retraction reflects tumour tethering of ducts/ligaments.

symptoms.symptom_name: Peau d'orange skin change (video-observable orange-peel dimpling)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peau d'orange reflects dermal lymphatic infiltration, characteristic of inflammatory/advanced disease.
Skin change can occur in infection too, but the painless afebrile context favours malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm, physically fixed 4 cm mass tethered to skin (clinician palpation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fixity and skin tethering are strong malignancy indicators — clinician-only sign, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Matted, fixed ipsilateral axillary lymphadenopathy (clinician palpation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Matted fixed nodes indicate regional metastatic spread — cannot be assessed remotely.
Non-specific constitutional symptom; in context raises concern for systemic burden.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever (afebrile on home thermometer), no rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/rigors makes an acute infective abscess less likely (though not fully excluded remotely).

symptoms.symptom_name: Not on anticoagulants or hormonal therapy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of anticoagulation makes an iatrogenic bleeding cause less likely, keeping a structural (tumour) cause of the discharge in focus.

investigation_details.expected_result: Spiculated mass, typically with malignant features
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed radiological confirmation and staging of a suspected malignancy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A spiculated mass on mammography strongly favours malignancy — but is a hospital/imaging investigation, never available in telehealth.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: No tissue diagnosis; cannot confirm malignancy or plan treatment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histology is the definitive confirmatory test — the AI can never obtain it.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fixed hard painless mass + bloody discharge + nipple retraction + peau d'orange + matted fixed nodes together is a near-classic advanced/inflammatory malignancy picture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent breast-clinic triple assessment (clinical examination + imaging + biopsy)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive pathway for a suspicious breast presentation; the AI arranges/recommends it but cannot perform any component.
Imaging to characterise the mass; anticipated spiculated lesion.
Assesses the mass and regional nodes and guides biopsy.
Tissue diagnosis — the only way to confirm malignancy and plan treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical oral antibiotics as a presumed-mastitis work-up substitute
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reflexively treating as infection would anchor on the wrong diagnosis and delay cancer work-up in an afebrile, painless, fixed-mass presentation.
